troubleshooting.title: Resolve a ParseError caused by missing front matter
troubleshooting.shortdesc: A ParseError means markdown-validator could not find a valid YAML front-matter block at the top of your file. Every Markdown file must begin with --- to be validated.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Fix the ParseError that occurs when a Markdown file does not begin with a valid YAML front-matter block delimited by ---.

condition.title: Situation
condition.p: You run validation and see an error similar to:
condition.codeblock: ParseError: docs/article.md — no front matter found. File must begin with a --- delimited YAML block.
condition.p: The file is not validated and counts as a failure in the report.

cause.title: Cause: Front matter block is absent
cause.p: The file does not start with ---, or the opening delimiter is preceded by whitespace or a blank line.

step.cmd: Ensure the very first line is exactly --- with no preceding whitespace, and close the block with a second --- line.

info.codeblock:
--- title: "Deploy a web application" ms.topic: tutorial ms.date: 2026-03-20 author: your-github-username description: "Step-by-step guide to deploying a web app." ---
# Tutorial: Deploy a web application ...

cause.title: Cause: File encoding issue
cause.p: The file contains a byte-order mark (BOM) or non-UTF-8 characters before the --- delimiter.

steps-informal.p: Save the file as UTF-8 without BOM. In most text editors, this is the default encoding. If you are using VS Code, check the encoding indicator in the status bar at the bottom right — it should show UTF-8.
